expectations.text: Uses the google-generativeai Python SDK (import google.generativeai)
expectations.evidence: import google.generativeai as genai

expectations.text: Reads GEMINI_API_KEY from environment variable or ~/scripts/ecosystem.env
expectations.evidence: Checks os.environ first, falls back to ~/scripts/ecosystem.env

expectations.text: Uses an appropriate Gemini model (Flash for cost efficiency or Pro for accuracy)
expectations.evidence: gemini-1.5-flash with comment 'Flash for cost efficiency per skill'

expectations.text: Sends transactions in a structured prompt and parses the categorized response
expectations.evidence: Structured prompt with category list, parses response.text.strip()

expectations.text: Includes error handling for API rate limits or failures
expectations.evidence: Try/except with 429/quota check, wait and retry logic

expectations.text: Uses Gemini's multimodal input (image + text prompt)
expectations.evidence: model.generate_content([prompt, image]) - sends both text and image

expectations.text: Loads an image file and sends it to the Gemini API
expectations.evidence: PIL.Image.open(image_path) then passed to generate_content

expectations.text: Prompts Gemini to extract structured data (merchant, date, total, line items)
expectations.evidence: Prompt requests merchant, date, total, line_items in JSON format

expectations.text: Parses the response into JSON format
expectations.evidence: json.loads(text) with code fence stripping

expectations.text: Uses the free tier model (Flash) as recommended by the skill
expectations.evidence: gemini-1.5-flash with comment 'Free tier per skill recommendation'

expectations.text: Uses the google-generativeai Python SDK (import google.generativeai)
expectations.evidence: Uses openai package instead. Mentions Gemini as alternative at end but doesn't use it.

expectations.text: Reads GEMINI_API_KEY from environment variable or ~/scripts/ecosystem.env
expectations.evidence: Reads OPENAI_API_KEY, not GEMINI_API_KEY

expectations.text: Uses an appropriate Gemini model (Flash for cost efficiency or Pro for accuracy)
expectations.evidence: Uses gpt-3.5-turbo, not a Gemini model

expectations.text: Sends transactions in a structured prompt and parses the categorized response
expectations.evidence: Sends structured prompt and parses response

expectations.text: Includes error handling for API rate limits or failures
expectations.evidence: No error handling or rate limit logic

expectations.evidence: Sends to OpenAI API, not Gemini

expectations.text: Prompts Gemini to extract structured data (merchant, date, total, line items)

expectations.evidence: json.loads on response content

expectations.text: Uses the free tier model (Flash) as recommended by the skill
expectations.evidence: Uses gpt-4-vision-preview, not Flash
